is an [intercity bus](/wiki/Intercity_bus_service "Intercity bus service") operator primarily serving the U.S. state of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"), with routes also serving [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin") and [Minnesota](/wiki/Minnesota "Minnesota"). Indian Trails is based in [Owosso, Michigan](/wiki/Owosso,_Michigan "Owosso, Michigan"), with offices in [Romulus](/wiki/Romulus,_Michigan "Romulus, Michigan"), [East Lansing](/wiki/East_Lansing,_Michigan "East Lansing, Michigan"), and [Kalamazoo](/wiki/Kalamazoo,_Michigan "Kalamazoo, Michigan").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

an express service connecting [East Lansing](/wiki/East_Lansing,_Michigan "East Lansing, Michigan"), [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Michigan "Brighton, Michigan"), and [Ann Arbor](/wiki/Ann_Arbor,_Michigan "Ann Arbor, Michigan") with [Detroit Metro Airport](/wiki/Detroit_Metropolitan_Airport "Detroit Metropolitan Airport"). The service makes twelve round trips daily, to be expanded to fourteen on August 25, 2025.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

a shuttle service which provides door-to-door transportation from Lansing-area addresses to the Michigan Flyer stop in downtown East Lansing. Operated by [Via Transportation](/wiki/Via_Transportation "Via Transportation"), Hytch uses minivans driven by independent contractors to provide rides.[[18]](#cite_note-18)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*] The service was available exclusively for ticketed Michigan Flyer passengers, who were able to add the service to their trip reservation for $10 extra per ride.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Hytch service was suspended on February 10, 2024.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Intercity bus operator based in Michigan

This article is about the bus company. For other uses, see [Indian Trails (disambiguation)](/wiki/Indian_Trails_(disambiguation) "Indian Trails (disambiguation)").

Indian Trails  
Indian Trails, Inc.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Indian Trails bus in [Pontiac, Michigan](/wiki/Pontiac,_Michigan "Pontiac, Michigan") | |
| Founded | 1910 |
| Headquarters | [Owosso, Michigan](/wiki/Owosso,_Michigan "Owosso, Michigan") |
| Locale | [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"), [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin"), [Minnesota](/wiki/Minnesota "Minnesota") |
| Service type | [Inter-city bus](/wiki/Coach_(vehicle)#Intercity_coach_service "Coach (vehicle)") |
| Annual ridership | 1 million+[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Website | [indiantrails.com](https://indiantrails.com) [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q6021774#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata") |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/22/Milwaukee_Wisconsin_7990.jpg/250px-Milwaukee_Wisconsin_7990.jpg)](/wiki/File:Milwaukee_Wisconsin_7990.jpg)

Indian Trails at [Milwaukee Intermodal Station](/wiki/Milwaukee_Intermodal_Station "Milwaukee Intermodal Station")

**Indian Trails** is an [intercity bus](/wiki/Intercity_bus_service "Intercity bus service") operator primarily serving the U.S. state of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"), with routes also serving [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin") and [Minnesota](/wiki/Minnesota "Minnesota"). Indian Trails is based in [Owosso, Michigan](/wiki/Owosso,_Michigan "Owosso, Michigan"), with offices in [Romulus](/wiki/Romulus,_Michigan "Romulus, Michigan"), [East Lansing](/wiki/East_Lansing,_Michigan "East Lansing, Michigan"), and [Kalamazoo](/wiki/Kalamazoo,_Michigan "Kalamazoo, Michigan").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Trails&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Indian Trails was founded in 1910 in Owosso by Wayne and [Cora Taylor](/wiki/Cora_V._Taylor "Cora V. Taylor") as the Phillips-Taylor Livery Service, whose main business was transporting cargo to and from [Durand Union Station](/wiki/Union_Station_(Durand) "Union Station (Durand)") and the surrounding [Shiawassee County](/wiki/Shiawassee_County "Shiawassee County"). Cora Taylor was the first woman in the United States to be issued a [chauffeur's](/wiki/Chauffeur "Chauffeur") license.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Beginning in the 1910s, the company expanded to include intercity offerings, becoming known as the Owosso-Flint Bus Line and by the 1930s served much of southern Michigan. Many of its buses ran along [US 12](/wiki/U.S._Route_12_in_Michigan "U.S. Route 12 in Michigan"), known as "the Old Indian Trail." Reflecting this, in 1935, the company took its current name, Indian Trails.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Indian Trails offers charters, casino trips and regular fixed-route daily service.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In 2006, Indian Trails assumed responsibility for a number of routes in Northern Michigan and the Upper Peninsula which [Greyhound Lines](/wiki/Greyhound_Lines "Greyhound Lines") had discontinued.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Indian Trails' daily scheduled bus service is part of Michigan's [Intercity Bus System](http://www.michigan.gov/documents/MDOT-PTD-intercitymap_78377_7.pdf) recognized by the Michigan Department of Transportation.

In November 2006, Indian Trails, in partnership with Okemos Travel, launched the Michigan Flyer, an express service connecting East Lansing, Jackson, and Ann Arbor with Detroit Metro Airport.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 2012, the Michigan Flyer absorbed the AirRide express service operated by [TheRide](/wiki/Ann_Arbor_Area_Transportation_Authority "Ann Arbor Area Transportation Authority"), and service to Jackson was discontinued.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) In October 2019, a new stop was added at the [Meijer](/wiki/Meijer "Meijer") store in [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Michigan "Brighton, Michigan").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

On March 1, 2023, Indian Trails ended its longtime operating agreement with [Greyhound Lines](/wiki/Greyhound_Lines "Greyhound Lines"), and introduced a new ticketing system.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13) The company also overhauled its route network, reintroducing direct service from [Detroit](/wiki/Detroit "Detroit") to [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), among other changes.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) Chicago service was subsequently discontinued in November 2023.[[16]](#cite_note-:0-16)

## Current routes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Trails&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Current routes")]

### Mainline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Trails&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Mainline")]

### Michigan Flyer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Trails&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Michigan Flyer")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/27/Michigan_Flyer_2020_Prevost_H3-45_3941_%28East_Lansing%29.jpg/250px-Michigan_Flyer_2020_Prevost_H3-45_3941_%28East_Lansing%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Michigan_Flyer_2020_Prevost_H3-45_3941_(East_Lansing).jpg)

Michigan Flyer coach in East Lansing

Indian Trails operates the **Michigan Flyer,** an express service connecting [East Lansing](/wiki/East_Lansing,_Michigan "East Lansing, Michigan"), [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Michigan "Brighton, Michigan"), and [Ann Arbor](/wiki/Ann_Arbor,_Michigan "Ann Arbor, Michigan") with [Detroit Metro Airport](/wiki/Detroit_Metropolitan_Airport "Detroit Metropolitan Airport"). The service makes twelve round trips daily, to be expanded to fourteen on August 25, 2025.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

#### Hytch

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Trails&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Hytch")]

In May 2023, Indian Trails introduced **Hytch,** a shuttle service which provides door-to-door transportation from Lansing-area addresses to the Michigan Flyer stop in downtown East Lansing. Operated by [Via Transportation](/wiki/Via_Transportation "Via Transportation"), Hytch uses minivans driven by independent contractors to provide rides.[[18]](#cite_note-18)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*] The service was available exclusively for ticketed Michigan Flyer passengers, who were able to add the service to their trip reservation for $10 extra per ride.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Hytch service was suspended on February 10, 2024.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### D2A2 and DAX

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Trails&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: D2A2 and DAX")]

Indian Trails operates the [D2A2](/wiki/D2A2 "D2A2") and [Detroit Air Xpress (DAX)](/wiki/Detroit_Air_Xpress "Detroit Air Xpress") public transit routes under contract with the [Regional Transit Authority of Southeast Michigan](/wiki/Regional_Transit_Authority_of_Southeast_Michigan "Regional Transit Authority of Southeast Michigan").
